title: Low class begins: ค ช ซ ท
pedagogy: Low-class consonants (1/5): paired low ค ช ซ ท — same sounds as high-class partners ข ฉ ส ถ, opposite tone behavior. Concepts: size of the low class; paired vs unpaired.

exercises.title: The low class — the big one
body: Twenty-four consonants — more than half the alphabet — are low class. This unit covers all of them.
The low class splits in two: paired letters that share a sound with a high-class partner, and unpaired sonorants (nasals, liquids, glides) with no partner.
The pairing matters: a high/low pair can spell every one of the five tones between them.

exercises.title: Same sound, different class
body: ค sounds exactly like ข — both say kh. But ข is high class and ค is low class, so the same written syllable carries a different tone.
This lesson's four letters all have a high partner: ค↔ข, ช↔ฉ, ซ↔ส, ท↔ถ.
When you meet a consonant that sounds like one you know, the class is the difference that matters.
